Name: curvature_routing
Title: Curvature-Aware Precision Routing for Efficient Neural Network Inference
Short Hypothesis: By incorporating curvature-aware precision routing and geometric subspace diagnostics, we can develop a framework that optimizes neural network inference efficiency while maintaining accuracy, thereby challenging existing assumptions about the trade-off between precision and performance.
Related Work: While existing work has explored precision routing and neural network optimization, our proposal distinguishes itself by integrating curvature-aware precision routing with geometric subspace diagnostics and deterministic optimization proxies, providing a more comprehensive and theoretically grounded approach. The lack of existing research in this specific area, as indicated by the empty search results, highlights the novelty and potential impact of our proposal.
Abstract: This project proposes a novel curvature-aware precision routing framework for efficient neural network inference. By combining geometric subspace diagnostics with deterministic optimization proxies, we aim to develop a theoretically grounded approach that optimizes precision routing for improved inference efficiency while maintaining accuracy. Our framework will be designed to operate within the constraints of a single GPU, ensuring feasibility and scalability. We will evaluate our approach using rigorous empirical validation, including systematic ablation protocols and multi-seed variance quantification, to demonstrate its effectiveness and robustness.
Experiments: Implement and evaluate the curvature-aware precision routing framework on a range of neural network architectures and datasets
Conduct ablation studies to quantify the impact of curvature-aware precision routing and geometric subspace diagnostics on inference efficiency and accuracy
Compare our approach to existing precision routing and neural network optimization techniques, using metrics such as inference time, accuracy, and computational overhead
Risk Factors and Limitations: The complexity of the curvature-aware precision routing framework may pose challenges for implementation and optimization
The reliance on geometric subspace diagnostics and deterministic optimization proxies may introduce additional computational overhead
The potential for overfitting or underfitting due to the precision routing framework's sensitivity to hyperparameters and dataset characteristics
